title: leafwax v0.2.0 — paleo-record workflow
text: Build plan extending leafwax package per-sample inversion (v0.1.0) full downcore paleo-record workflow claim-taxonomy assessment, supporting manuscript revision bradleylab/leafwax-spatial. Branch: feat/v10-posteriors--paleo-workflow.

title: leafwax v0.2.0 — paleo-record workflow
text: working paleohydrologist can hand leafwax published δ²H_wax record (downcore series + age model + site coordinates) claimed hydroclimate interpretation, get back: within-record δ²H_precip posterior trajectory full uncertainty propagation. defensibility verdict: level (1–4) claim taxonomy original interpretation actually holds. Diagnostics: σ_within stationary baseline interval, lag-1 temporal autocorrelation ρ_t, local effective slope site, change-detection threshold conditions. package becomes operational artifact Sections 4.5.3, 4.5.5, 4.5.6 manuscript (σ_within obligation, slope ceiling stationarity, four-level claim taxonomy). manuscript references package; package implements manuscript demands.

title: leafwax v0.2.0 — paleo-record workflow
text: invert_d2H() / invert_d2h() — per-sample inversion engine. load_posteriors() — reads inst/extdata/posteriors/<model>_posterior.rds. predict_spatial_mpp() — Gaussian-process kriging knot effects new location (modified Predictive Process). available_models(), model_details(), select_best_model() — model metadata routing. 14 model variants extdata (older fit; needs replacement, see Phase F).

title: leafwax v0.2.0 — paleo-record workflow
text: v10 posteriors leafwax_gca_working/results/c2_run_20260501/<model>/posterior_draws.rds draws_array (iterations × chains × variables). Package expects draws_df (rows = draws, cols = named parameters). Conversion posterior::as_draws_df(d) — straightforward. v10 model uses 125 knots; package extdata 120-knot fit. New <model>_knots.rds files needed. v10 parameter names match package code reads: beta_0, beta_oipc, sigma, lambda_decay, effective_scale_km, ls_intercept_km, ls_slope_km, sigma_intercept_spatial, sigma_slope_spatial, plus z_intercept_spatial[1..125] z_slope_spatial[1..125] spatial draws (mu[1..N] fitted values, drop conversion). non-spatial models (baseline, baseline_env, baseline_veg, full, full_interact) spatial knot params; posterior fewer columns. <model>_knots.rds produced .

what: Phase F — v10 posterior packaging (prerequisite, ~1 day)
title: leafwax v0.2.0 — paleo-record workflow
text: Goal: every v10 model loadable via load_posteriors() API break downstream code. Deliverables: Iterates 14 model directories <gca-working>/results/c2_run_20260501/. Reads posterior_draws.rds draws_array. Subsets global parameters package consumes (beta_0, beta_oipc, sigma, lambda_decay, effective_scale_km, ls_intercept_km, ls_slope_km, sigma_intercept_spatial, sigma_slope_spatial spatial models; plus β coefficients vegetation/elevation models). spatial models, also extracts z_intercept_spatial[] z_slope_spatial[] =1..125 draws--knots matrix. Converts draws_df; writes inst/extdata/posteriors/<model>_posterior.rds. Writes 125-knot Fibonacci coordinates inst/extdata/spatial_metadata/<model>_knots.rds spatial models. Knots model-independent (Fibonacci sphere across spatial variants), can single shared object — keep per-model file pattern code reads doesn’t change. Update inst/extdata/spatial_models_metadata.json reflect 125-knot fits v10 lineage. Update inst/extdata/data_info.rds load_posteriors() reports v10 lineage. Smoke test: library(leafwax); res <- invert_d2H(d2h_wax = -180, d2h_wax_sd = 3, longitude = -90, latitude = 38, model_name = "baseline_sp") runs returns credible-interval result close v10 paper reports test point. New tests tests/testthat/test-v10-posteriors.R covering 14 models load + single inversion runs + spatial models give different results non-spatial input.

what: Phase A — σ_within + downcore time series (~1 day)
title: leafwax v0.2.0 — paleo-record workflow
text: Goal: -site time series gets one consistent spatial draw user-supplied σ_within instead global posterior σ. API additions: sigma_within supplied, replace residual σ posterior predictive sigma_within (sample Normal(sigma_within, sigma_within_sd) per draw supplied). record_id non-NULL value appears across rows, sample spatial GP per draw site reuse across series, rather redrawing per row. Default behavior: baseline_interval NULL, treat full record baseline (warn user conflates real variability noise). explicit baseline_interval, subset estimate σ detrended residuals optional AR1 correction (σ_eff = σ_naive × √(1 - ρ²)). Returns named list: σ_within (point estimate), σ_within_se, n_baseline, ρ_t_baseline, method. Tests: σ_within < σ_global synthetic stationary series. σ_within → 0 perfectly autocorrelated series AR1 correction. record_id constancy gives reduced reconstruction variance vs per-row spatial draws.

what: Phase B — local effective slope (~0.5 day)
title: leafwax v0.2.0 — paleo-record workflow
text: Goal: paleohydrologist can extract model’s site-specific slope posterior override defended local value, capped α=0.88 default. API additions: Returns per-draw slope vector site combining global beta_oipc posterior spatial slope GP prediction (z_slope_spatial projected via predict_spatial_mpp). override supplied (single value per-draw vector), use instead model’s slope. ceiling non-NULL, truncate draw ceiling. Warn >5% draws truncated (suggests user’s defended slope model inconsistent simple-model bound). invert_d2H(..., slope = NULL) — accept either NULL (use model slope), numeric value (use fixed point estimate), vector length n_draws (use per-draw). Tests: Slope local_effective_slope matches hand-rolled extraction baseline_sp. Ceiling truncation reports count truncated draws. Override propagates correctly invert_d2H.

what: Phase C — change detection with autocorrelation (~0.5 day)
title: leafwax v0.2.0 — paleo-record workflow
text: Goal: given downcore record + reconstruction posterior, report within-record threshold δ²H_precip change detection user-chosen confidence levels. API additions: Returns posterior probability ΔδD_precip baseline_interval test_intervals element exceeds user-supplied magnitudes. Reports 95% within-record detection threshold using formula §4.5.3 manuscript (1.96 × √(2(1-ρ_t)) × √(σ_within² + σ_analytical²) / β_eff). estimate_temporal_autocorrelation(d2h_wax, age, method = c("ar1", "lomb_scargle")) — handles unevenly sampled series. Tests: ρ_t recovery synthetic AR(1) series. Threshold sensitivity matches curves Figure 5.

title: leafwax v0.2.0 — paleo-record workflow
text: Goal: given record + claim spec, return Level 1–4 verdict itemize ’s defended vs missing. API additions: evidence string), seasonal_source_stationary, etc. Runs inversion reconstruction NULL. L1: change exceeds analytical + σ_within? L2: alternatives excluded corroborating_proxies? L3: defended slope + σ_within + propagated uncertainty? L4: independent stationarity evidence supplied? Returns highest level claim survives , itemized pass/fail reasons. Tests: corroborating speleothem reaches Level 4. synthetic record 5‰ wax shift 2 samples corroboration drops Level 1.

title: leafwax v0.2.0 — paleo-record workflow
text: vignettes/paleo-record-workflow.Rmd — step--step real Iso2k record. Recommended first example: Lake Malawi (LS11KOMA) — Common Era, ~3‰ within-record SD, makes marginal-claim case concretely. Optional second example contrast: Lake N3 (LS16THN301) — 8 kyr Holocene, 35‰ within-record SD, real signal. already extracted /tmp/leafwax_records/<dataset>__d2H.csv. vignette walks: 1. Read downcore series. 2. Estimate σ_within stationarity-defended baseline interval. 3. Extract / defend local effective slope. 4. Run invert_d2H() record_id, sigma_within, slope set. 5. Run detect_change() magnitudes claimed original publication. 6. Run assess_claim() published claim spec. 7. Plot reconstructed δ²H_precip within-record uncertainty.

title: Paleo-record workflow: from a downcore series to a defensible claim
text: estimate_sigma_within() computes within-record residual SD stationarity-defended baseline interval. Manuscript Section 4.5.3 explains global posterior sigma (~16 per mil) overstates relevant noise change detection inside single record. Lake Malawi, full Common-Era record reasonably stationary treat entire 11-sample series baseline. function warn conflates real climate variability noise – accepted illustration; real application defend sub-interval independent grounds. within-record SD ~3 per mil linear detrending characteristic Common-Era leaf-wax records.
code: sw <- suppressWarnings(estimate_sigma_within(   d2h_wax       = malawi$d2h_wax,   age           = malawi$age,   baseline_interval = NULL,   # full record (warning)   detrend       = "linear",   ar1_correction = TRUE )) sw #> $sigma_within #> [1] 2.439809 #>  #> $sigma_within_se #> [1] 0.5455578 #>  #> $sigma_naive #> [1] 2.552037 #>  #> $n_baseline #> [1] 11 #>  #> $rho_t_baseline #> [1] -0.2932885 #>  #> $method #> [1] "baseline_interval=full record (WARNING); detrend=linear; ar1_correction=TRUE"

title: Paleo-record workflow: from a downcore series to a defensible claim
text: local_effective_slope() returns per-draw vector d2H_wax-d2H_precip slope site, combining global posterior beta_oipc spatial slope GP. default ceiling 0.88, simple-model upper bound manuscript Section 4.5.5. defended slope point estimate typically posterior median; keep per-draw vector inversion propagates slope uncertainty.
code: malawi_lon <- 34.1878 malawi_lat <- -10.0183  slope_post <- suppressWarnings(local_effective_slope(   longitude   = malawi_lon,   latitude    = malawi_lat,   model_name  = "baseline_sp",   n_draws     = 200,   ceiling     = 0.88,   verbose     = FALSE ))  quantile(slope_post, c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975)) #>      2.5%       50%     97.5%  #> 0.4632060 0.5776181 0.7080186

what: 4. Bayesian inversion with sigma_within and slope
title: Paleo-record workflow: from a downcore series to a defensible claim
text: invert_d2H() accepts slope vector built plus within- record SD. pass record_id function validates rows one site, ask full posterior draws matrix can hand detect_change(). returned summary one row per sample posterior median credible-interval bounds; posterior_draws n_iter x n_samples matrix used .
code: recon <- suppressWarnings(invert_d2H(   d2H_wax    = malawi$d2h_wax,   d2H_wax_sd = rep(3, nrow(malawi)),   longitude  = rep(malawi_lon, nrow(malawi)),   latitude   = rep(malawi_lat, nrow(malawi)),   model_name = "baseline_sp",   n_posterior_draws = 200,   sigma_within = sw$sigma_within,   slope        = slope_post,   record_id    = "LS11KOMA",   return_full  = TRUE,   verbose      = FALSE ))  head(recon$summary[, c("d2h_wax", "d2h_precip_median",                        "d2h_precip_lower", "d2h_precip_upper")]) #>    d2h_wax d2h_precip_median d2h_precip_lower d2h_precip_upper #> 1 -102.962          62.61846         29.98491        105.05846 #> 2 -101.987          65.23026         28.40409        102.54416 #> 3 -105.778          57.76140         25.52705         95.84459 #> 4 -107.577          55.47606         19.82396         86.73767 #> 5 -104.848          58.98257         26.62906         95.63846 #> 6 -106.813          55.44942         25.66746         94.91055

what: 5. Detection threshold and posterior probability of change
title: Paleo-record workflow: from a downcore series to a defensible claim
text: detect_change() reports manuscript Section 4.5.3 detection threshold posterior probability difference mean d2H_precip two intervals exceeds magnitude. split record 1000 yrBP ask whether 30 per mil shift d2H_precip across boundary. threshold smallest d2H_precip change two independent samples site can distinguished within-record noise 95% confidence. sigma_within ~3 per mil threshold comparatively small; actual record’s posterior delta typically smaller still, point example.
code: rho_t <- estimate_temporal_autocorrelation(   malawi$d2h_wax, malawi$age, method = "ar1" )  dc <- detect_change(   reconstruction    = recon,   age               = malawi$age,   baseline_interval = c(0, 1000),   test_intervals    = list(post_1000 = c(1000, 2000)),   sigma_within      = sw$sigma_within,   sigma_analytical  = 3,   rho_t             = rho_t,   beta_eff          = stats::median(slope_post),   confidence        = 0.95,   magnitudes        = c(10, 30, 50) )  dc$threshold #> [1] 14.4393 dc$intervals #>    interval n_baseline n_test delta_mean delta_median delta_lower delta_upper #> 1 post_1000          6      5  -7.626347    -7.690567   -14.66225  -0.7655204 #>   p_abs_delta_gt_10 p_abs_delta_gt_30 p_abs_delta_gt_50 #> 1              0.29                 0                 0

title: Paleo-record workflow: from a downcore series to a defensible claim
text: assess_claim() walks four-level taxonomy manuscript Section 4.5.6. test hypothetical claim record shows 30 per mil drying signal across 1000 yrBP, supported corroborating evidence stationarity arguments. taxonomy walks level turn: every level highest one passed reported reason failed. Reading levels data frame top bottom recommended way see record-claim pair supports.
code: malawi_record <- data.frame(   d2h_wax     = malawi$d2h_wax,   age         = malawi$age,   d2h_wax_err = rep(3, nrow(malawi)) )  claim <- list(   level             = 4,                       # asserted level   interval_baseline = c(0, 1000),   interval_test     = c(1000, 2000),   sigma_within      = sw$sigma_within,   sigma_analytical  = 3,   rho_t             = rho_t,   confidence        = 0.95,   beta_eff          = stats::median(slope_post),   magnitude_precip  = 30,   corroborating_proxies = list(     speleothem_d18O = "regional speleothems show coeval shift"   ),   vegetation_stationary = list(     value    = TRUE,     evidence = "n-alkane chain length distributions stable across the boundary"   ),   seasonal_source_stationary = list(     value    = TRUE,     evidence = "regional speleothem d18O shows no seasonality shift"   ),   evapotranspirative_stationary = list(     value    = TRUE,     evidence = "leaf-water proxy stable; no aridity transition"   ) )  verdict <- suppressWarnings(assess_claim(   record         = malawi_record,   claim          = claim,   reconstruction = recon ))  verdict$highest_level #> [1] 0 verdict$asserted_supported #> [1] FALSE verdict$levels #>   level passed                                                      summary #> 1     1  FALSE delta_wax = -4.27 permil; 95% threshold = 8.34 permil (FAIL) #> 2     2  FALSE                                   blocked by Level 1 failure #> 3     3  FALSE                                   blocked by Level 2 failure #> 4     4  FALSE                                   blocked by Level 3 failure

title: Bayesian Inversion of Leaf Wax d2H to Precipitation d2H
text: downcore series, workflow combines five functions: full sequence real Iso2k record vignette("paleo-record-workflow", package = "leafwax").
code: library(leafwax)  # 1. Within-record residual SD on a stationarity-defended interval sw <- estimate_sigma_within(   d2h_wax = record$d2h_wax,   age     = record$age,   baseline_interval = c(0, 5000),   detrend = "linear",   ar1_correction = TRUE )  # 2. Per-draw local slope at the site, with the simple-model ceiling slope <- local_effective_slope(   longitude  = -90,   latitude   = 38,   model_name = "baseline_sp",   ceiling    = 0.88 )  # 3. Inversion with within-record residual + defended slope recon <- invert_d2H(   d2H_wax    = record$d2h_wax,   d2H_wax_sd = record$d2h_wax_err,   longitude  = rep(-90, nrow(record)),   latitude   = rep( 38, nrow(record)),   model_name = "baseline_sp",   sigma_within = sw$sigma_within,   slope        = slope,   record_id    = "your_record_id",   return_full  = TRUE )  # 4. Detection threshold + posterior P(change > magnitude) rho_t <- estimate_temporal_autocorrelation(record$d2h_wax, record$age) dc <- detect_change(   reconstruction    = recon,   age               = record$age,   baseline_interval = c(0, 5000),   test_intervals    = list(post = c(5000, 10000)),   sigma_within      = sw$sigma_within,   rho_t             = rho_t,   beta_eff          = stats::median(slope),   confidence        = 0.95,   magnitudes        = c(10, 30, 50) )  # 5. Four-level taxonomy verdict on a published claim verdict <- assess_claim(   record         = record,   claim          = list(level = 4, ...),     # see ?assess_claim   reconstruction = recon ) verdict$highest_level

what: Assess a paleoclimate claim against the leaf-wax taxonomy — assess_claim
title: Assess a paleoclimate claim against the leaf-wax taxonomy — assess_claim
text: Walks four-level taxonomy manuscript Section 4.5.6 reports highest level claim survives . taxonomy : Level 1: leaf-wax delta-2-H change occurred two intervals. Defensible change exceeds analytical uncertainty within-record residual SD sigma_within. Level 2: wax change consistent directional hydroclimate change. Requires corroborating evidence (multi- proxy concordance, sedimentological context, biomarker evidence vegetation stability) supplied via corroborating_proxies. Level 3: wax change implies quantitative delta-2-H_precip magnitude. Requires defended local effective slope, defended sigma_within, explicit uncertainty propagation inversion. reconstruction NULL function calls invert_d2H() . Level 4: magnitude uniquely attributable precipitation isotope change rather vegetation, source-water seasonality, evapotranspirative enrichment. Requires independent stationarity evidence non- precipitation control interval.

title: Within-record d2H_precip change detection — detect_change
code: detect_change(   reconstruction,   age,   baseline_interval,   test_intervals = NULL,   sigma_within,   sigma_analytical = 3,   rho_t = NULL,   beta_eff,   confidence = 0.95,   magnitudes = NULL )

title: Within-record d2H_precip change detection — detect_change
text: reconstruction Output invert_d2H(..., return_full = TRUE) downcore series. Must contain posterior_draws matrix shape n_iter x n_samples. age Numeric vector, length n_samples, sample ages matching reconstruction columns. baseline_interval Length-2 numeric c(min, max) defining baseline window age units. test_intervals Either length-2 numeric vector single test window, named list length-2 numerics multiple windows. NULL skips per-interval probability table returns threshold. sigma_within Numeric, required, within-record residual SD leaf-wax per mil (typically estimate_sigma_within()). sigma_analytical Numeric, analytical uncertainty d2H_wax measurements per mil (default 3). rho_t Numeric, lag-1 temporal autocorrelation. Use estimate_temporal_autocorrelation() compute. Defaults 0 (independent samples) message. beta_eff Numeric, local effective slope. Use local_effective_slope() point estimate (e.g., median). confidence Numeric (0, 1), confidence level detection threshold. Default 0.95. magnitudes Optional numeric vector magnitudes (per mil) evaluate posterior Pr(|delta| > magnitude) .

title: Within-record d2H_precip change detection — detect_change
text: list elements: threshold - detection threshold d2H_precip requested confidence level. formula - components used: z, rho_t, sigma_within, sigma_analytical, beta_eff. intervals - data frame one row per test interval reporting posterior median CI delta (magnitudes supplied) posterior probability exceeding magnitude.

title: Within-record d2H_precip change detection — detect_change
text: $$\mathrm{threshold}_{precip} =       \frac{z_{\alpha/2}\, \sqrt{2(1 - \rho_t)}\,             \sqrt{\sigma_{within}^2 + \sigma_{analytical}^2}}            {\beta_{\mathrm{eff}}}$$ threshold smallest difference d2H_precip two independent samples can distinguished within-record noise chosen confidence level.

title: Estimate the within-record residual SD of a leaf-wax record — estimate_sigma_within
text: Estimate within-record residual standard deviation sigma_within stratigraphic baseline interval downcore leaf-wax delta-2-H record. Within-record residual variance generally smaller global posterior sigma, spatial structure cancels -site sources variance (laboratory, vegetation background, basin size, integration timescale) vary inside single record. Manuscript Section 4.5.3 explains frames obligations user.

title: Estimate the within-record residual SD of a leaf-wax record — estimate_sigma_within
code: estimate_sigma_within(   d2h_wax,   age,   baseline_interval = NULL,   detrend = c("none", "linear", "loess"),   ar1_correction = TRUE )

title: Estimate the within-record residual SD of a leaf-wax record — estimate_sigma_within
text: d2h_wax Numeric vector leaf-wax delta-2-H measurements (per mil). age Numeric vector sample ages (monotone time-like variable; length d2h_wax). baseline_interval Length-2 numeric c(min, max) defining baseline window age units. NULL means use full record (warning emitted). detrend One "none" (default), "linear", "loess", describing remove trends within baseline computing residuals. "linear" fits lm(d2h_wax ~ age). "loess" fits loess(d2h_wax ~ age, span = 0.75). ar1_correction Logical (default TRUE); TRUE, applies AR(1) variance reduction described .

title: Estimate the within-record residual SD of a leaf-wax record — estimate_sigma_within
text: list elements: sigma_within - point estimate (per mil), AR(1)-corrected requested. sigma_within_se - asymptotic standard error returned sigma_within. sigma_naive - sample SD residuals AR(1) correction. n_baseline - number samples used. rho_t_baseline - lag-1 temporal autocorrelation residuals baseline. method - one-line description choices made.

title: Estimate the within-record residual SD of a leaf-wax record — estimate_sigma_within
text: function operates baseline interval . baseline_interval = NULL, entire record treated baseline warning emitted: conflates real climate variability measurement process noise, returned sigma_within upper bound rather defended estimate. Real use specify stratigraphic interval stationarity vegetation, hydrology, source-water seasonality can defended independent grounds. Within baseline window, function: subsets record baseline, optionally detrends ("linear" "loess") remove long-wavelength trends user want absorbed residual SD, computes lag-1 temporal autocorrelation (rho_t) residuals, returns naive standard deviation residuals (sd(residuals)) AR(1)-corrected effective SD ar1_correction = TRUE. correction sigma_eff = sigma_naive * sqrt(1 - rho_t^2) |rho_t| < 1, conservative reduction accounts variance sequential samples share. standard error sigma_within approximated sigma_within / sqrt(2 * (n_baseline - 1)), asymptotic SE normal-distribution sample SD.

title: Estimate the within-record residual SD of a leaf-wax record — estimate_sigma_within
code: # \donttest{ # Synthetic stationary baseline: noise around a flat mean set.seed(1) n  <- 80 ag <- seq(0, 8000, length.out = n) d  <- -160 + rnorm(n, 0, 5) est <- estimate_sigma_within(d, ag,                              baseline_interval = c(0, 4000),                              detrend = "none",                              ar1_correction = TRUE) est$sigma_within #> [1] 4.430219 # }

title: Estimate lag-1 temporal autocorrelation — estimate_temporal_autocorrelation
text: Estimate lag-1 autocorrelation rho_t leaf-wax record's residuals flat-mean detrend, ordering age. quantity enters within-record detection threshold manuscript Section 4.5.3 (Var(X1 - X2) = 2 sigma^2 (1 - rho_t)).

title: Estimate lag-1 temporal autocorrelation — estimate_temporal_autocorrelation
text: d2h_wax Numeric vector leaf-wax delta-2-H measurements (per mil). age Numeric vector sample ages, length d2h_wax. method One "ar1" "lomb_scargle".

title: Estimate lag-1 temporal autocorrelation — estimate_temporal_autocorrelation
text: Numeric scalar [-1, 1], NA_real_ residuals constant (e.g., n < 3 finite samples).

title: Estimate lag-1 temporal autocorrelation — estimate_temporal_autocorrelation
text: Two methods supported: "ar1" (default): Pearson correlation resid[-n] resid[-1] age-ordering. irregularly sampled series approximation; see "lomb_scargle" alternative. "lomb_scargle": yet implemented. Returns error pointing user "ar1" spectral implementation lands. plan estimate rho_t dominant timescale Lomb-Scargle periodogram irregularly sampled series.

title: Estimate lag-1 temporal autocorrelation — estimate_temporal_autocorrelation
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1) n <- 200 rho <- 0.7 e  <- numeric(n); e[1] <- rnorm(1, 0, 5) for (k in 2:n) e[k] <- rho * e[k-1] + rnorm(1, 0, 5 * sqrt(1 - rho^2)) ag <- seq(0, 10000, length.out = n) estimate_temporal_autocorrelation(-150 + e, ag) #> [1] 0.6923911 # }

Use estimate_sigma_within() obtain stationary baseline interval record. Section 4.5.3 manuscript explains global posterior sigma overstates within-record uncertainty. sigma_within_sd Numeric, optional standard error sigma_within. non-NULL, posterior draw resamples residual SD Normal(sigma_within, sigma_within_sd) (truncated zero), uncertainty within-record SD propagates prediction. record_id Character numeric, optional record identifier. supplied constant across input rows, rows treated belonging downcore series: spatial Gaussian process evaluated per posterior draw shared site, spatial draws reused across series rather redrawn per row. current implementation already shares spatial draws identical (longitude, latitude) pairs; record_id argument adds explicit validation caller intends within-record inference. slope Optional numeric override d2H_wax-d2H_precip slope. NULL (default) uses model's site-specific slope, .e., beta_oipc plus spatial slope GP perturbation site. single numeric replaces slope fixed point estimate (broadcast across posterior draws). vector length n_draws used per draw. text: longitude Numeric, single longitude decimal degrees. latitude Numeric, single latitude decimal degrees. model_name Character, v10 model name (see available_models()). Must spatial model (*_sp) site-specific slope differ global mean; non-spatial models return global posterior unchanged. override Optional numeric. NULL (default) uses model slope. single value broadcasts across draws. vector length n_draws used per draw. ceiling Optional numeric upper bound slope. Default 0.88, simple-model ceiling stationarity. Set Inf NULL disable. n_draws Integer, optional number posterior draws use (NULL uses ). title: Local effective slope at a paleo-reconstruction site — local_effective_slope
text: Three modes: Default: returns model's per-draw slope site. Override (single value per-draw vector) replaces model slope defended local value (e.g., independent evidence source-water seasonality, leaf-water enrichment, vegetation). Ceiling: draw exceeding ceiling (default 0.88, simple-model upper bound epsilon_app ~= -120 permil) truncated ceiling. warning emitted 5\ user's intended interpretation inconsistent simple-model bound. Pass returned vector invert_d2H(..., slope = ...) propagate inversion.

what: Major release: paleo-record workflow + v10 calibration
title: leafwax 0.2.0
text: leafwax 0.2.0 makes package operational backend manuscript “Spatial modeling improves calibration leaf wax hydrogen isotopes precipitation” (Bradley, Geochimica et Cosmochimica Acta). package now ships v10 posterior draws 14 hierarchical Bayesian models reported manuscript exposes four-phase paleo workflow manuscript references Sections 4.5.3, 4.5.5, 4.5.6.

previous_headings: Major release: paleo-record workflow + v10 calibration
title: leafwax 0.2.0
text: estimate_sigma_within(d2h_wax, age, baseline_interval, detrend, ar1_correction) – estimate within-record residual SD stationarity-defended baseline interval. Manuscript Section 4.5.3. local_effective_slope(longitude, latitude, model_name, override, ceiling = 0.88, n_draws) – per-draw local slope site, simple-model ceiling manuscript Section 4.5.5. estimate_temporal_autocorrelation(d2h_wax, age, method) – lag-1 autocorrelation within-record detection threshold. detect_change(reconstruction, age, baseline_interval, test_intervals, sigma_within, sigma_analytical, rho_t, beta_eff, confidence, magnitudes) – within-record d2H_precip detection threshold posterior probability change. assess_claim(record, claim, reconstruction, ...) – walks four-level taxonomy manuscript Section 4.5.6 returns highest level claim survives , itemized pass/fail reasons.

previous_headings: Major release: paleo-record workflow + v10 calibration
title: leafwax 0.2.0
text: New args sigma_within, sigma_within_sd, record_id, slope. sigma_within enters leaf-wax per-mil units propagates beta_oipc_eff like measurement uncertainty (combined quadrature standardized wax space inversion). record_id validates input rows one site flags coordinate inconsistency constant identifier. slope accepts NULL (model slope), single point estimate, per-draw vector; rejects zero / negative / non-finite values. exported wrapper now forwards return_full, credible_level, verbose.

previous_headings: Major release: paleo-record workflow + v10 calibration
title: leafwax 0.2.0
text: available_models() exposes 14 v10 model names manuscript: baseline, baseline_sp, baseline_env, baseline_env_sp, baseline_veg, baseline_veg_sp, full, full_sp, full_interact, full_interact_sp, elevation_only_sp, elevation_c4_sp, elevation_c4_interact_sp, c4_only_sp. load_posteriors() derives capability flags (has_c4, has_pft, has_elevation, has_gp, has_interaction) posterior column names rather name regexes; full, full_sp, full_interact, full_interact_sp correctly report vegetation interaction effects. load_posteriors() subsamples deterministically (stratified thinning), two calls model_name n_draws return draws subset. lets local_effective_slope(..., n_draws = N) pair position invert_d2H(..., n_posterior_draws = N, slope = ...).
